calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-28-2025 Vacated

Motion To Compel Five9, Inc.S Compliance With Deposition And Document Subpoena; Request For Contempt And Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-13-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: fAppearances (by Zoom):
Kimberly Arouh, Esq. (619) 994-3993 Buchanan for Defendant.
Anna Landa, Esq. 818-990-0888 Law Offices of Kenneth Freed for Other party Five9, Inc.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT SENIOR HEALTHCARE ADVISORS LLC AKA SENIOR HEALTHCARE ADVISORS' Motion To Compel Five9, Inc.S Compliance With Deposition And Document Subpoena; Request For Contempt And Monetary Sanctions.

Defendant Senior Healthcare Advisors' motion to compel third party Five9 to appear for deposition is granted and its request for monetary sanctions is denied. Five9 must appear for deposition on a mutually agreeable date no later than March 7, 2025 and produce all non-privileged documents requested. In the event Five9 contends that any requested documents are privileged, Five9 must idenity all such documents in a privilege log. Senior Healthcare has shown good cause for the deposition and document production. Monetary sanctions are denied because Five9 did not lack substantial justification in failing to attend a deposition just prior to an ex parte hearing on a request to continue the trial where Senior Healthcare did not communicate its intent to go forward with the deposition notwithstanding the close proximity in time to the ex parte hearing and the parties dispute whether there was an understanding whether deposition dates were to be continued due to the ex parte application.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed form of order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and must drop a hard copy of the proposed order in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/HEK)
